title: Meridian 2.5 to 3.0 Runtime Readiness Backlog
principle: Meridian 3.0 is a competence milestone, not a feature milestone.
definition_of_done: I can open Meridian like a normal app, talk to Prime, run local commands, attach context, recover sessions, inspect skills, restart the bridge, and understand failures without opening PowerShell.

milestones.name: Real Runtime Foundation
milestones.goal: Make Meridian launch, supervise, diagnose, and repair its own local runtime without requiring a terminal.

items.title: App-owned bridge supervisor
acceptance_criteria: Launching the Meridian executable starts the model bridge automatically if absent.
Closing PowerShell does not kill Meridian.
Bridge process lifecycle is owned by the app runtime, not the user's shell.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed Electron app-owned bridge tests
packaged EXE launch smoke reached healthy bridge without terminal dependency
bridge stopped after packaged app process was stopped
items.remaining_work: Keep packaged launch and shutdown ownership in V3 release smoke.

items.title: Bridge health state machine
acceptance_criteria: UI shows bridge states: starting, healthy, degraded, and offline.
Bridge status includes last observed error and retry state.
Status updates without requiring a full app restart.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed bridge health startup/retry/state tests
packaged EXE health smoke returned state healthy and version local-bridge-routes-v4
items.remaining_work: Keep healthy/starting/degraded/unreachable cases covered in Electron runtime tests.

items.title: Restart bridge control
acceptance_criteria: User can restart the bridge from Meridian UI without touching PowerShell.
Restart action reports progress and final status.
Failed restart shows the blocking reason and next repair step.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed supervised respawn tests
packaged /bridge/restart smoke returned supervised restart and recovered healthy bridge with a new PID
items.remaining_work: Keep /restart-bridge visible result covered by command smoke.

items.title: App runtime PATH normalization
items.proof_command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "$snapshot = Get-ChildItem $env:APPDATA -Recurse -Filter meridian-model-bridge-runtime-env.json -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1; if (-not $snapshot) { Write-Error 'runtime snapshot not found; start Meridian and retry'; exit 1 }; Get-Content $snapshot.FullName | ConvertFrom-Json | Select-Object pathEnvKey, originalPathSegmentCount, normalizedPathSegmentCount, appendedSegments | Format-List"
acceptance_criteria: Meridian can find Codex CLI from the packaged app environment.
Meridian does not show a false Codex-not-installed error when Codex works in the user's shell.
Resolved tool paths are visible in diagnostics.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed bridgeRuntimePath PATH normalization tests
runtime env visibility is persisted from Electron-owned bridge launch
items.remaining_work: Keep runtime PATH snapshot visible in startup diagnostics.

items.title: Startup diagnostics pass
acceptance_criteria: On launch, Meridian verifies bridge reachability, Codex CLI, auth visibility, writable storage, and skill registry.
Failures are captured before the first user message.
Startup diagnostics produce a compact user-facing report.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed startup diagnostics tests for PATH, storage, auth, and skill registry states
diagnostics now treat missing/partial skill registry rows as explicit warning/failure states instead of silent startup ambiguity
items.remaining_work: Keep startup diagnostics compact and actionable in UI copy.

items.title: Diagnostic repair actions
acceptance_criteria: Each failed check has a clear repair action.
Supported repair actions include restart bridge, recheck CLI, open logs, and copy diagnostic report.
Repair actions update the health state after completion.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed runtime diagnostics and restart proof paths
packaged restart smoke proves supervised repair path for bridge restart
items.remaining_work: Add or verify direct UI repair actions for CLI path recheck, log open/copy, and startup retry beyond bridge restart.

milestones.name: Session And Command Competence
milestones.goal: Make sessions durable and make local commands execute locally before model routing.

items.title: Durable session store
acceptance_criteria: Sessions persist across app restart.
Sessions persist across bridge restart.
Session records include enough metadata to restore the visible session list.
evidence_refs: sessions are visible/recoverable in current runtime
user still observed possible missing Codex session
items.remaining_work: Prove survival across app restart, bridge restart, and abnormal close.

items.title: Session recovery flow
acceptance_criteria: Interrupted or incomplete sessions appear under recoverable sessions.
Recovered session entries include timestamp and last message summary.
User can reopen a recovered session without manual file inspection.
evidence_refs: session recovery UI exists
missing-session scenario still needs traceable audit trail
items.remaining_work: Add visible session provenance and recovery audit events.

items.title: Local slash command router
acceptance_criteria: /clear, /status, /skills, and /restart-bridge are handled before model routing.
Local command execution does not wait for a model response.
Unknown slash commands produce a local explanation instead of a model timeout.
evidence_refs: local slash commands are intercepted before model routing
/status and /debug run without prompt send
items.remaining_work: Keep command handler covered by regression tests.

items.title: /clear clears visible session window
acceptance_criteria: Running /clear immediately clears the active visible transcript.
/clear does not call the model.
Clearing the visible window does not delete durable session history unless a separate destructive command is explicitly implemented.
evidence_refs: /clear is handled locally before model routing
visible prompt/session clearing behavior restored
items.remaining_work: Keep clear behavior separate from model context clearing until model-context reset is explicit.

items.title: Local command registry
acceptance_criteria: Commands have name, description, authority level, owner harness, and execution path metadata.
Registry distinguishes executable local commands from informational capability rows.
Registry can be rendered in the skills panel.
evidence_refs: local command registry is exposed to UI/runtime context
model-facing command explanations were noisy and needed persona cleanup
items.remaining_work: Keep command registry discoverable without forcing it into every model answer.

items.title: Model command awareness context
acceptance_criteria: Prime can explain available local commands.
Prime does not claim to execute UI commands directly.
Model context includes command descriptions and local execution boundaries.
evidence_refs: Prime receives runtime command awareness
Prime over-reported commands until persona/context cleanup
items.remaining_work: Move command awareness behind relevance rules and test answer tone.

milestones.name: Context Intake
milestones.goal: Make copied, pasted, uploaded, and attached user context reliably visible to Prime.

items.title: Paste text support
acceptance_criteria: Text pasted into Spark input appears reliably.
Pasted text sends normally through the active message flow.
Paste behavior works in the packaged app runtime.
evidence_refs: prompt text entry and send path were restored after prompt lockup fixes
items.remaining_work: Keep Enter/send behavior in smoke coverage.

items.title: Paste image support
acceptance_criteria: Pasted images appear as removable attachment chips.
Pasted images are passed to the bridge as context.
Prime can inspect or discuss a pasted image in the same session.
evidence_refs: pasted image attachment support is implemented and tested
bridge context attachment tests pass
items.remaining_work: Add end-to-end model-visible image proof when model image routing is enabled.

items.title: File upload support
acceptance_criteria: User can upload images, markdown, text, JSON, CSV, logs, PDFs, DOC, and DOCX files.
Uploaded files appear as removable attachment chips.
Upload errors explain size or type limits clearly.
evidence_refs: Prime upload control is wired to the Prime attachment tray
commit deb51d235 preserves the Upload control across tray rerenders
items.remaining_work: Keep regression that rerendering pending attachments does not remove Upload or file input.

items.title: Attachment materialization
acceptance_criteria: Bridge saves attachments to request-scoped local files.
Saved attachment paths are passed to the Codex or model prompt.
Attachment files are bounded by count and size limits.
evidence_refs: attachments are represented as pending context chips
materialization path has UI/test coverage
items.remaining_work: Prove uploaded docs/images reach the bridge payload and are recoverable in debug logs.

items.title: Context payload contract
acceptance_criteria: Every model call includes prompt, visible session context, persona, command registry, attachments, and app or bridge status.
Context payloads are explicit enough for Prime to know what is available.
Payload contract avoids implying authority the runtime does not have.
evidence_refs: context payload contract exists for Prime attachments
needs stronger proof for all supported file types
items.remaining_work: Add payload inspection proof for image, markdown, txt, json, csv, pdf, and docx where supported.

items.title: Attachment smoke tests
acceptance_criteria: Automated smoke covers pasted image reaching bridge context.
Automated smoke covers uploaded document reaching bridge context.
Smoke tests run against the real UI path, not only isolated helpers.
evidence_refs: focused attachment tests and packaged UI smoke pass
full upload-to-model context smoke is still open
items.remaining_work: Create one named smoke covering paste, upload, send, and model-visible context.

milestones.name: Skills And Prime
milestones.goal: Make skills discoverable and make Prime consistent in the actual response path.

items.title: Skills panel data model
acceptance_criteria: Panel can represent local commands, Meridian capabilities, Codex skills, project skills, and Graphify.
Every row has a stable source and authority level.
The data model supports both executable and informational entries.
evidence_refs: skills panel work exists and local/model skills were added
user observed empty/collapsible behavior regressions during iteration
items.remaining_work: Lock data model with tests for local commands, local skills, Codex skills, and bridge metadata.

items.title: Collapsible skill groups
acceptance_criteria: Skills panel groups are collapsible.
Skills panel search works across all groups.
Skills panel is populated when opened.
evidence_refs: skills grouping was requested and partially implemented
collapsible behavior still needs verified UI proof
items.remaining_work: Prove all model/local skill groups expand/collapse and preserve search results.

items.title: Skill source clarity
acceptance_criteria: Each skill shows name, description, source, authority level, and whether it is executable or informational.
Rows are not clickable as commands unless a real command path exists.
Debug source fields are hidden unless diagnostics mode is active.
evidence_refs: source labels and display-only confusion were identified
copy still needs product-language cleanup
items.remaining_work: Replace harness labels with user-facing source/provenance labels.

items.title: Prime persona contract
acceptance_criteria: Prime is defined as a role speaking through Spark on behalf of Meridian.
Prime contract includes directives, proof obligations, and response preferences.
Prime response posture favors progress, confidence, and ownership without false claims.
evidence_refs: Prime persona contract is present in the Prime harness UI
Prime wording was humanized after robotic runtime context responses
items.remaining_work: Keep persona visible as a collapsible Prime panel item.

items.title: Persona injected into real model path
acceptance_criteria: Actual Codex or model calls receive the Prime contract consistently.
Identity questions are answered as Prime through Spark on behalf of Meridian.
Persona injection is covered by tests or prompt payload proof.
evidence_refs: persona payload wiring was repaired after primeContractPayload failure
needs golden prompt proof across providers
items.remaining_work: Add prompt payload snapshot tests proving persona reaches the real model path.

items.title: Persona regression tests
acceptance_criteria: Golden tests cover identity questions.
Golden tests cover failure handling and ownership tone.
Golden tests prevent false runtime authority claims.
evidence_refs: persona/static harness tests exist
tone regression needs output-level fixtures
items.remaining_work: Add golden response fixtures for role, command-awareness, and ownership tone.

items.title: Remove harness leakage from user UX
acceptance_criteria: User-facing panels do not lead with display-only or registry jargon unless diagnostics mode is active.
Capability labels are rewritten as user-understandable state.
Internal advisory language remains available where it protects authority boundaries.
evidence_refs: several noisy harness/status surfaces were hidden or collapsed
debug/status copy still needs a real product pass
items.remaining_work: Replace display-only/harness wording with Beacon/Relay/Prime product language.

milestones.name: Runtime Readiness Gate
milestones.goal: Block 3.0 until Meridian behaves like a real app and passes end-to-end readiness smoke tests.

items.title: Packaged executable smoke
acceptance_criteria: Fresh launch from EXE starts UI and bridge with no terminal required.
Bridge health reaches healthy or explains the blocker.
Packaged smoke can be repeated after app restart.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed and produced dist/Meridian-0.1.0-x64.exe
packaged EXE launch smoke reached healthy bridge without opening PowerShell
bridge shut down after the app process was stopped
items.remaining_work: Repeat this smoke on the release candidate artifact before tagging 3.0.

items.title: End-to-end message smoke
acceptance_criteria: User sends a message.
Prime responds.
Bridge status remains healthy through the exchange.
evidence_refs: message input/send path has been restored
model timeout path still needs clearer recovery behavior
items.remaining_work: Gate on first prompt, timeout recovery, second prompt, and visible elapsed time.

items.title: End-to-end command smoke
acceptance_criteria: /clear works locally.
/status works locally.
/restart-bridge works locally.
/skills opens or explains the skills panel locally.
evidence_refs: local command router tests cover /clear, /status, /skills, /debug, and /restart-bridge before model routing
packaged /bridge/restart smoke recovered the local bridge through supervised restart
items.remaining_work: Keep command smoke in the V3 proof gate.

items.title: End-to-end context smoke
acceptance_criteria: Uploaded document is visible to Prime in one session.
Pasted image is visible to Prime in one session.
Attachment paths and metadata are visible in bridge proof.
evidence_refs: Prime Upload button survival is smoke-tested
full context-to-model proof remains open
items.remaining_work: Gate paste image, upload document, upload image, and send with debug payload evidence.

items.title: Session survival smoke
acceptance_criteria: Close app.
Reopen app.
Previous session is recoverable.
evidence_refs: session disappearance concern remains unresolved
items.remaining_work: Gate app close/reopen, bridge restart, Codex session recovery, and session provenance audit.

items.title: Failure explainability smoke
acceptance_criteria: Simulated missing bridge error produces actionable UI repair steps.
Simulated missing Codex error produces actionable UI repair steps.
Failure message identifies the failed layer.
evidence_refs: npm run proof:v3 passed startup diagnostics failure/warning tests
packaged restart smoke proves bridge repair path
items.remaining_work: Still needs UI proof for missing Codex, unavailable skill registry, and timeout explanation copy from the user-facing panel.

items.title: Version truth audit
acceptance_criteria: 3.0 is blocked until runtime readiness gates pass.
Release notes distinguish harness capability maturity from runtime competence.
No broad feature expansion is allowed to bypass failed P0 readiness gates.
evidence_refs: runtime foundation and command gates now have V3 proof evidence
model-response, context-to-model, skills UX, and session survival gates still block 3.0 truth audit
items.remaining_work: Do not mark 3.0 final until M30-002, M30-004, M30-005, and remaining partial P0/P1 gates are proven or explicitly deferred.

status_note: Status reflects runtime-ui checkpoint work through commit b8e86f6c3 plus V3 proof gate evidence: npm run proof:v3, packaged EXE health smoke, and supervised restart smoke. Model-response, context-to-model, skills UX, and session-survival gates remain open until proven end to end.
